data.title: Assessing Rockwool (CPSE:ROCK B) Valuation After Launch Of €150m Share Buy-Back Programme
data.description:
Advertisement

Share buy-back puts Rockwool (CPSE:ROCK B) in focus

Rockwool (CPSE:ROCK B) has moved into the spotlight after launching a share buy-back programme of up to €150m. Recent disclosures show 4,786,356 B shares held, equal to 2.26% of share capital.

See our latest analysis for Rockwool.

The new buy-back comes against a softer share price backdrop, with a 30-day share price return showing a 5.26% decline and a 1-year total shareholder return showing a 14.64% decline. The 3-year total shareholder return of 8.26% suggests longer term gains have been modest rather than dramatic, which hints that sentiment has cooled recently even as management commits fresh capital to repurchases at around DKK213.55 per share.

If Rockwool’s buy-back has you thinking about where capital is quietly at work elsewhere, it could be a good moment to check out fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With Rockwool’s shares down 14.64% over the past year and trading around DKK213.55, while analysts see value closer to DKK236.06, is this a mispriced insulation specialist, or is the market already factoring in future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 14.6% Undervalued

With Rockwool last closing at DKK213.55 against a narrative fair value of DKK250.06, the most followed storyline sees clear upside, built on gradual earnings and utilization gains rather than a quick rebound.

Ongoing and near-term capacity expansions in key growth markets (U.S., Romania, India, West Coast U.S., France), including new electrified production lines, position Rockwool to capitalize on surging demand from tighter building codes and energy efficiency mandates, supporting both top-line growth and higher utilization-driven margin leverage over the medium

to long-term.

Read the complete narrative.

Want to see what kind of revenue runway and steady margins have to line up for that DKK250.06 fair value to add up? The narrative leans on measured growth, unchanged profitability, and a modest future earnings multiple that still implies room for rerating. Curious how those pieces fit together over several years rather than just the next few quarters?

Result: Fair Value of DKK250.06 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, the story can change quickly if construction demand stays weak for longer, or if new capacity leaves Rockwool with underused plants and pressured margins.

Find out about the key risks to this Rockwool narrative.

Another View: Cash Flows Send A Different Signal

While the consensus narrative points to a fair value of DKK250.06 and labels Rockwool as undervalued, our DKK based SWS DCF model tells a very different story. It shows a fair value estimate of just DKK87.33 per share, which implies the shares screen as expensive on that basis.

When one method points to upside and the DCF points to downside, it raises a simple question for you as an investor: are earnings based stories or longer term cash flow assumptions the version of Rockwool you trust more?

Look into how the SWS DCF model arrives at its fair value.

Simply Wall St performs a discounted cash flow (DCF) on every stock in the world every day (check out Rockwool for example). We show the entire calculation in full. You can track the result in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted when this changes, or use our stock screener to discover 868 undervalued stocks based on their cash flows. If you save a screener we even alert you when new companies match - so you never miss a potential opportunity.

Build Your Own Rockwool Narrative

If the story here does not quite match your view, or you prefer to test the assumptions yourself, you can build a full Rockwool thesis, compare scenarios, and put your own numbers to work in just a few minutes with Do it your way.

A good starting point is our analysis highlighting 3 key rewards investors are optimistic about regarding Rockwool.

Looking for more investment ideas?

If Rockwool has caught your attention, do not stop here. Put a few more ideas on your radar so you are not relying on just one storyline.

  • Spot potential value opportunities early by scanning these 868 undervalued stocks based on cash flows that may trade below what their cash flows imply.
  • Tap into long term technology shifts by checking out these 25 AI penny stocks shaping how artificial intelligence reaches real world applications.
  • Add a different growth angle by reviewing these 18 cryptocurrency and blockchain stocks tied to digital assets, payments, and blockchain infrastructure.

data.title: The Bull Case For JCDecaux (ENXTPA:DEC) Could Change Following CFL’s 10-Year Digital Rail Contract - Learn Why
data.description:
• JCDecaux SE announced it has been awarded a 10-year exclusive contract to operate the advertising assets of Luxembourg’s national railway company CFL, including a fully digital network of 143 new high-efficiency screens across stations nationwide from June 2026.
  • This deal deepens JCDecaux’s presence in a country that combines a large daily commuter base with a high concentration of European institutions and multinational firms, offering advertisers a tightly targeted transport media platform.
  • We’ll examine how this long-term, fully digital rail advertising contract with CFL could influence JCDecaux’s investment narrative and growth focus.

The best AI stocks today may lie beyond giants like Nvidia and Microsoft. Find the next big opportunity with these 25 smaller AI-focused companies with strong growth potential through early-stage innovation in machine learning, automation, and data intelligence that could fund your retirement.

Advertisement

What Is JCDecaux's Investment Narrative?

For JCDecaux, the big picture an investor needs to buy into is a mature, asset-heavy media business that is using digitisation and programmatic technology to gradually improve the quality of its earnings rather than chasing breakneck expansion. The CFL win in Luxembourg fits into that story as another long-dated, fully digital contract that reinforces visibility on revenue, but on its own is unlikely to move the needle on short term results, especially given the stock’s relatively modest recent gains and already full earnings multiple versus peers. Where it may matter is on the risk side: a growing portfolio of exclusive, high-capex transport and retail media concessions tightens JCDecaux’s dependence on advertising cycles and debt-funded investments at a time when its balance sheet is already a watchpoint.

However, one key financial risk here is easy to underestimate at first glance. JCDecaux's share price has been on the slide but might be up to 48% below fair value. Find out if it's a bargain.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Three Simply Wall St Community fair value views span roughly €11.28 to €19.11, underlining how far apart individual investors can be. Set against JCDecaux’s expanding digital concession base, this spread hints at very different expectations for how effectively the group turns long-term contracts into resilient earnings.

data.title: How Investors May Respond To Alkane Resources (ASX:ALK) Record Output, Cash Flow and Nagambie Earn‑In
data.description:
• Alkane Resources recently reported record quarterly gold equivalent production of 43,663 ounces for the December 2025 quarter, generating site operating cash flow of A$133,000,000 across its Costerfield, Tomingley and Björkdal operations.
  • The company also agreed a conditional placement and earn-in with Nagambie Resources over a nearby gold‑antimony tenement package, underscoring a push to test deeper mineralisation potential around its Costerfield hub.
  • We’ll now examine how record production and cash generation, alongside the Nagambie earn-in, shape Alkane’s investment narrative for investors.

The end of cancer? These 29 emerging AI stocks are developing tech that will allow early identification of life changing diseases like cancer and Alzheimer's.

Advertisement

What Is Alkane Resources' Investment Narrative?

For Alkane, I think the big picture is simple: you have to believe the company can turn solid, multi‑mine gold and antimony output into consistently stronger cash generation, without eroding that progress through cost blowouts or dilution. The record December‑quarter production and A$133,000,000 in site cash flow clearly support the near‑term production story and help underpin the current guidance range, so they look like a meaningful positive for the short‑term catalyst list. The earn‑in with Nagambie fits the same theme: it pushes harder on the Costerfield growth option set, but also nudges exploration and execution risk higher, particularly given Alkane’s already rich earnings multiple and thinner margins versus last year. With the share price already well ahead of the broader market, setbacks on costs or drilling could matter more from here.

However, one risk around high expectations and valuation is easy to overlook at first. Alkane Resources' shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued by 24%. Find out what it's worth.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Five Simply Wall St Community fair value views span A$1.01 to a very large outlier above A$500, highlighting how far opinions can stretch. Against that backdrop, the recent record quarter and Nagambie earn‑in sharpen the focus on whether Alkane can convert current momentum into sustained, higher quality earnings.

data.title: TTM Technologies’ Raytheon Deal And Index Entry Raise Valuation Questions
data.description:
• TTM Technologies (NasdaqGS:TTMI) has entered a multi year supply agreement with Raytheon for critical defense hardware valued at up to US$200 million.
  • The agreement supports Raytheon’s LTAMDS program through advanced electronic components used in key defense systems.
  • TTM Technologies is also set to join the S&P MidCap 400 index, which may draw fresh attention from index linked and institutional investors.

TTM Technologies focuses on printed circuit boards and related electronic components for sectors such as aerospace and defense, networking, and industrial applications. The new Raytheon agreement places the company more deeply inside long term defense programs that often rely on stable, high reliability supply partners.

For investors, the combination of a multi year contract and S&P MidCap 400 inclusion may provide more consistent visibility into potential demand, together with higher awareness from fund managers. It is worth watching how TTM Technologies executes on this contract and how its index inclusion affects trading volumes and ownership over time.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for TTM Technologies by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on TTM Technologies.

data.title: The Bull Case For Kongsberg Gruppen (OB:KOG) Could Change Following Major Polish SAN CUAS Contract Expansion
data.description:
• Kongsberg Defence & Aerospace and consortium partner Polska Grupa Zbrojeniowa recently signed a NOK 16.00 billion contract to deliver 18 SAN CUAS counter-drone batteries to the Polish Armaments Agency, integrating guns, missiles, interceptor drones and tailored command-and-control systems into Poland’s air defence network.
  • An important aspect of this agreement is KONGSBERG’s plan to expand manufacturing capacity in Poland, deepening industrial ties while leveraging its PROTECTOR family of weapon systems as the core technology for the CUAS solution.
  • We’ll now explore how this large SAN CUAS contract and planned manufacturing expansion in Poland shapes Kongsberg Gruppen’s broader investment narrative.

The end of cancer? These 29 emerging AI stocks are developing tech that will allow early identification of life changing diseases like cancer and Alzheimer's.

Advertisement

What Is Kongsberg Gruppen's Investment Narrative?

To own Kongsberg Gruppen, you need to be comfortable backing a defence and maritime technology group that is already delivering sizeable revenue and earnings, and is priced at a premium to many peers. The NOK 16.00 billion SAN CUAS contract with Poland looks material in that context, potentially reinforcing near-term growth visibility and supporting the existing thesis around high-quality earnings and strong return on equity, even if the precise financial impact is not yet reflected in consensus numbers. The planned manufacturing expansion in Poland also nudges capacity and execution risk higher, at a time when expectations embedded in the share price already look demanding and the stock has rerated sharply in recent months. Meanwhile, the small buyback tied to incentive plans hardly moves the capital-allocation needle.

However, the premium valuation and rising execution demands are not something investors can ignore. Kongsberg Gruppen's shares are on the way up, but they could be overextended by 23%. Uncover the fair value now.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Nine Simply Wall St Community fair value estimates span roughly NOK 267.80 to an extremely high NOK 1,997.79, underlining just how far opinions diverge. When you set that against a premium earnings multiple and fresh execution risk from the SAN CUAS ramp up, it is clear you are weighing very different readings of the same story.

data.title: Japan Ethylene Decarbonization Alliance Could Be A Game Changer For Mitsubishi Chemical Group (TSE:4188)
data.description:
• Asahi Kasei, Mitsui Chemicals, and Mitsubishi Chemical Group have formed a joint operating entity to decarbonize and consolidate ethylene production in western Japan, backed by selection for METI’s Fiscal 2025 HtA Support Program and a ¥21.20 billion investment plan including subsidies.
  • A particularly interesting aspect is the planned use of Asahi Kasei’s Revolefin™ technology to produce bioethanol-based, lower-carbon ethylene and propylene, potentially reshaping how basic chemicals are sourced across multiple industries.
  • We will now examine how this decarbonization alliance, underpinned by government-supported investment and technology deployment, influences Mitsubishi Chemical Group’s investment narrative.

The end of cancer? These 29 emerging AI stocks are developing tech that will allow early identification of life changing diseases like cancer and Alzheimer's.

Advertisement

What Is Mitsubishi Chemical Group's Investment Narrative?

For Mitsubishi Chemical Group, you really have to believe in a complex turnaround story where portfolio restructuring, earnings recovery and disciplined capital allocation matter more than fast top line growth. The new ethylene decarbonization alliance slots into that narrative as a long-dated, capital-intensive project that could support relevance in basic chemicals, but it is unlikely to move the needle on the key near term catalysts that investors are watching: delivery against lowered FY2026 guidance, execution on the sizeable buyback, and progress on portfolio moves like potential divestments. With the shares already pricing in very high earnings multiples and profit margins still thin, the alliance arguably adds another execution layer to an already busy agenda rather than easing current pressures. It tilts the risk mix a bit further toward long term project and balance sheet discipline.

However, investors also need to weigh one specific balance sheet concern that is easy to overlook. Despite retreating, Mitsubishi Chemical Group's shares might still be trading 10% above their fair value. Discover the potential downside here.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Three Simply Wall St Community members currently see fair value between ¥905.45 and ¥1,279.92, reflecting very different expectations. Against that backdrop, the decarbonization alliance and ongoing restructuring raise real questions about execution risk and how much earnings improvement is already priced in, which readers may want to explore in more detail.

data.title: Novo Nordisk Oral Wegovy Launch Reshapes Obesity Access And Valuation Outlook
data.description:
• Novo Nordisk (NYSE:NVO) has launched an FDA approved oral version of its obesity drug Wegovy, expanding its GLP 1 obesity treatment portfolio.
  • The company is working with consumer outlets such as Amazon and Costco to widen distribution and patient access.
  • Novo Nordisk plans to integrate Catalent's U.S. fill finish site to roughly double its domestic supply chain capacity for obesity treatments.

Novo Nordisk, known for its diabetes and obesity treatments, now adds an oral Wegovy option that sits alongside its injectable GLP 1 drugs. For you as an investor, this introduces a different access point for patients who prefer pills over injections, in a market where treatment convenience can significantly influence adoption.

The broader push into channels such as Amazon and Costco, together with the planned expansion of U.S. fill finish capacity via Catalent, provides NYSE:NVO with additional ways to reach patients and manage supply. If these moves result in steadier product availability and broader coverage, they could become key factors to watch in the company’s long term obesity strategy.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Novo Nordisk by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Novo Nordisk.

How Novo Nordisk stacks up against its biggest competitors

Advertisement

Quick Assessment

  • ✅ Price vs Analyst Target: At US$59.43 versus a consensus target of about US$64.63, the price sits roughly 8% below analyst expectations, leaving some headroom to that level.
  • ✅ Simply Wall St Valuation: Simply Wall St flags the shares as trading about 60.5% below its estimate of fair value, which is a wide discount.
  • ✅ Recent Momentum: A 30 day return of about 16.8% shows strong recent momentum into the oral Wegovy news.

Check out Simply Wall St's in depth valuation analysis for Novo Nordisk.

Key Considerations

  • 📊 The FDA approved oral Wegovy and broader retail access could widen the obesity patient pool and support the existing GLP 1 franchise.
  • 📊 Keep an eye on obesity prescription volumes, U.S. supply ramp from the Catalent site and how pricing evolves as access expands.
  • ⚠️ With 3 flagged risks, including dividend sustainability and higher debt, balance the growth story against balance sheet and earnings quality checks.

Dig Deeper

data.title: Is It Time To Reconsider Progress Software (PRGS) After Its 1 Year Share Price Slump?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Progress Software is attractively priced or not, this article will walk through what the numbers are really saying about its current valuation.
  • The stock closed at US$40.92, with returns of 3.6% decline over 7 days, 4.7% decline over 30 days, 0.4% decline year to date, 28.6% decline over 1 year, 26.2% decline over 3 years, and 1.7% gain over 5 years. This gives you a sense of how sentiment around risk and opportunity has shifted over different timeframes.
  • Recent coverage of Progress Software has focused on its role as a provider of application development and infrastructure software, as investors reassess where it fits within the broader software sector. This context matters because moves in the share price are often tied to how investors feel about the resilience and relevance of its product portfolio.
  • On Simply Wall St's valuation checklist, Progress Software scores a 5 out of 6 for being assessed as undervalued. Next, we will walk through the main valuation methods behind that score and then finish with a way to look at value that goes beyond any single model.

Find out why Progress Software's -28.6% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Progress Software Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A DCF model takes estimates of a company’s future cash flows and discounts them back to today using a required return, aiming to translate those future dollars into a single present value per share.

For Progress Software, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $228.5 million. Analysts provide several years of explicit forecasts, and Simply Wall St then extends those projections, with free cash flow for 2030 estimated at $334.35 million and further ten year projections stepping up modestly from there.

Putting all of those projected cash flows together, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about US$89.94 per share. Compared with the recent share price of US$40.92, this implies the stock is trading at about a 54.5% discount to that DCF estimate. This indicates a material valuation gap based on these cash flow assumptions.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Progress Software is undervalued by 54.5%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 868 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Progress Software.

Approach 2: Progress Software Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like Progress Software, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of current earnings. Investors usually accept higher P/E ratios when they expect stronger earnings growth or see lower risk, and look for lower P/E ratios when growth expectations are more modest or risks feel higher.

Progress Software currently trades on a P/E of 23.56x. That sits below both the Software industry average P/E of 28.00x and the peer average of 27.27x, which suggests the market is applying a lower earnings multiple than it does to many comparable names.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Progress Software is 24.46x. This is a proprietary estimate of what the P/E might be given factors such as the company’s earnings growth profile, its industry, profit margins, market cap and specific risks. Because it adjusts for these company level traits, the Fair Ratio can be more tailored than a simple comparison with peers or the broad industry, which treat all companies as if they deserve similar multiples.

With the current P/E of 23.56x versus a Fair Ratio of 24.46x, Progress Software appears modestly undervalued on this earnings based check.

Result: UNDERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1417 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Progress Software Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce you to Narratives, a simple way to connect your view of Progress Software’s business with the numbers behind it.

A Narrative is your story for the company, where you spell out what you think its future revenue, earnings and margins might look like, and then link that forecast to a fair value estimate.

On Simply Wall St, Narratives sit inside the Community page and are designed to be easy to use. This means you can move from story, to forecast, to fair value without needing to build your own spreadsheet.

Narratives help you compare each Narrative’s Fair Value to the current share price. They automatically refresh when new information such as news or earnings is added to the platform.

For example, one Progress Software Narrative might assume a much higher fair value than the current price. Another might assume a lower fair value than the current price. This shows how different investors can look at the same company and reach very different conclusions.

Do you think there's more to the story for Progress Software? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: Glencore Lithium Deal Deepens Battery Supply Role In UK And Europe
data.description:
• Glencore subsidiary agrees a five year lithium hydroxide offtake deal with Alkemy Capital Investments' planned Teesside refinery in the UK.
  • The agreement secures battery grade lithium chemicals supply for Glencore, targeting customers across the UK and wider European battery sector.
  • The move expands Glencore's role in the battery raw materials chain as demand grows for materials linked to EVs and energy storage.

For investors watching LSE:GLEN, this new offtake deal comes with the shares around £4.9925 and a 22.1% return year to date. The stock is also up 46.5% over the past year and 146.5% over five years, highlighting how the market has reacted over time to Glencore's wider commodities exposure and portfolio moves.

The lithium agreement gives Glencore a foothold in the battery chemicals segment, tied to UK and European electrification plans. For shareholders, it adds another building block in the company’s energy transition story, with long term supply from a domestic refinery relevant to how its role in EV and storage value chains may evolve.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Glencore by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Glencore.

How Glencore stacks up against its biggest competitors

The lithium hydroxide offtake agreement ties Glencore more tightly to the electric vehicle and energy storage supply chain by securing up to 10,000 tonnes per year of battery grade material from Alkemy’s planned Teesside refinery from early 2028. For a group that still earns a large share of cash flow from coal and base metals like copper and zinc, this move supports its battery raw materials offering in the UK and Europe and sits alongside its wider production footprint in copper, nickel and cobalt where peers such as Rio Tinto and BHP are also active.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Glencore Narrative

The deal lines up with investor narratives that focus on Glencore’s role in electrification and energy transition, adding a downstream chemicals angle to its existing metals production and marketing activities. It also complements the company’s copper guidance and production profile, where management has been highlighting long term copper growth options and cost initiatives, by showing that Glencore is looking across the value chain rather than only at mine output.

Risks and Rewards Around This Deal

  • Secures potential access to 25,000 to 50,000 tonnes of lithium hydroxide over five years, which may help Glencore serve battery and EV customers more consistently in the UK and Europe.
  • Supports Glencore’s efforts to be seen as an important supplier of battery raw materials alongside larger copper and nickel positions, which some investors already view as a key reward in the long term story.
  • Volumes and timing are tied to Alkemy’s Teesside project delivering on its front end engineering work and final investment decision, so execution risk on the refinery could affect the contract’s value.
  • Analysts have flagged broader risks for Glencore around earnings volatility, regulation and balance sheet pressures, which could influence how much benefit investors ascribe to new offtake agreements like this one.

What To Watch Next

From here, watch for Alkemy reaching final investment decision on Teesside, any updates from Glencore on marketing arrangements into UK and European battery customers, and how this fits alongside copper, nickel and cobalt guidance as the Rio Tinto merger discussions play out and competitors such as Anglo American adjust their own battery materials plans. If you want to see how different investors are thinking about Glencore’s transition, check community narratives in the full set of GLEN stories.

data.description:

In the near term, the key catalysts remain production stability, cost control and progress on the large share repurchase plan, all of which now carry more weight given the higher production run rate. data.title: SouthState Bank Integration Of Independent Bank Starts To Shape Returns
data.description:
• SouthState Bank (NYSE:SSB) reports that it has successfully completed the integration of Independent Bank.
  • The company highlights that the acquisition is contributing to earnings and tangible book value growth in its latest quarter.
  • Management points to smooth systems conversions and regulatory approvals as key milestones in the integration process.

SouthState Bank, a regional banking institution, is using the Independent Bank acquisition to deepen its presence in existing markets and extend into new ones. For you as an investor, the headline is that management links current quarter earnings and tangible book value performance directly to this completed integration. That positions this deal as an active driver of the bank's current financial profile, rather than solely a future plan.

Leadership, including CEO John Corbett, describes the completed integration as a base for future business development and momentum in newly entered areas. As you evaluate NYSE:SSB, this event offers a fresh data point on how the bank handles large-scale M&A, systems conversions, and regulatory coordination, all of which can influence risk, growth opportunities, and capital deployment choices over time.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for SouthState Bank by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on SouthState Bank.

How SouthState Bank stacks up against its biggest competitors

For SouthState, tying the Independent Bank integration to both earnings and tangible book value effects indicates that this deal is already feeding into the income statement and balance sheet rather than sitting as idle goodwill. With full year 2025 net interest income of US$2,303.31m and net income of US$798.67m, plus active capital actions such as a US$0.60 quarterly dividend and a multi year buyback authorization, the bank is using the completed integration as a platform for a more returns focused business model in a peer set that includes names like Truist and Regions Financial.

Advertisement

How this fits the SouthState Bank narrative

The update aligns with the existing SouthState narrative that emphasizes expansion in higher growth markets, M&A execution and efficiency gains as earnings drivers. Management linking the Independent Bank integration to loan and deposit momentum supports that story of combining acquired scale with organic production, while the ongoing repurchases and dividend indicate the board is comfortable returning capital alongside that growth plan.

Risks and rewards to keep in mind

  • 🎁 Integration progress tied to higher net interest income and earnings provides evidence that prior acquisition plans are flowing through to reported results.
  • 🎁 The active buyback program and cash dividend suggest the board is focused on capital return alongside balance sheet growth.
  • ⚠️ Net charge offs of US$10.5m, or 0.09% of average loans, highlight credit risk that investors may want to watch, especially given exposure to commercial real estate noted in prior narratives.
  • ⚠️ Analysts have highlighted regulatory and funding cost pressures as key risks, which could matter more as the balance sheet grows through acquisitions and organic loan production.

What to watch next

From here, it may be useful to watch whether the combined bank can keep credit costs in check while maintaining loan and deposit growth and continuing buybacks under the new authorization through 2027. For additional context on how this integration fits into longer term growth, risk and capital return stories, you can review community narratives for SouthState Bank on the company's dedicated page.

data.title: Is Hexcel (HXL) Still Attractively Priced After Recent Aerospace Sector Attention?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Hexcel at around US$82.81 still offers value, it helps to first see how its recent share price behaviour lines up with what the fundamentals suggest.
  • The stock has had a mixed short term patch, with a 1.6% decline over the last 7 days, a 12.1% return over 30 days, a 7.7% return year to date, a 28.5% return over 1 year, a 22.1% return over 3 years, and an 80.6% return over 5 years.
  • Recent news around Hexcel has focused on the company as part of broader discussions on aerospace and advanced materials suppliers. This has kept investor attention on how it is positioned in that space and provides context for the share price moves as investors react to sector level themes and reassess what they are willing to pay for the stock.
  • On our valuation checks, Hexcel currently scores 2 out of 6 for being undervalued. We will unpack this using different valuation methods before finishing with a more rounded way to think about what the stock is really worth.

Hexcel scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Hexcel Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting them back to today using an appropriate rate. It is essentially asking what Hexcel’s future cash generation is worth in today’s dollars.

For Hexcel, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow stands at about $139.5 million. Analyst estimates and extrapolations point to projected free cash flow of around $496.0 million in 2035, with intermediate years ranging from $203.1 million in 2026 to $309.5 million in 2028, all in dollars and discounted back to present value.

Bringing these projected cash flows together, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $96.92 per share. Compared with the recent share price of around $82.81, the model implies Hexcel trades at roughly a 14.6% discount to this estimate, which indicates the shares may be undervalued on this measure.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Hexcel is undervalued by 14.6%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 868 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Hexcel.

Approach 2: Hexcel Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like Hexcel, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand because it links what you pay directly to the earnings the business is generating today. It helps you see how many dollars the market is willing to pay for each dollar of earnings.

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends on how quickly earnings are expected to grow and how risky those earnings are. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher multiple, while lower growth or higher risk usually points to a lower, more conservative P/E.

Hexcel currently trades on a P/E of 60.26x. That is close to the peer average of 60.44x, and above the broader Aerospace & Defense industry average of 41.63x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Hexcel is 31.94x. This Fair Ratio is a proprietary estimate of what a reasonable P/E could be for the company, given its earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and specific risks.

Compared with simple peer or industry comparisons, the Fair Ratio aims to be more tailored to Hexcel’s own characteristics rather than relying on broad group averages. With the current P/E of 60.26x sitting well above the Fair Ratio of 31.94x, this indicates that the shares look expensive on this metric.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1417 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Hexcel Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce you to Narratives, which are essentially your story about Hexcel, expressed through your own view of fair value and assumptions for future revenue, earnings and margins.

A Narrative links what you believe about the business to a simple financial forecast, and then to an estimated fair value that you can compare directly with Hexcel’s current share price to decide whether it looks attractive, fully valued or expensive to you.

On Simply Wall St, Narratives sit inside the Community page and are designed to be easy to use. You can adjust assumptions in a few clicks and see how your fair value view changes as new information such as news or earnings updates is reflected in the data.

For Hexcel, for example, one investor might build a Narrative that supports a relatively high fair value per share, while another might use more cautious assumptions and arrive at a much lower fair value. Seeing those side by side can help you decide which story you find more convincing.

Do you think there's more to the story for Hexcel? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: Leidos Targets 2030 With ENTRUST Deal And OpenAI AI Push
data.description:
• Leidos Holdings (NYSE:LDOS) announced the acquisition of ENTRUST Solutions Group, expanding its reach in energy infrastructure services.
  • The company also entered a partnership with OpenAI to integrate generative and agentic AI tools into core government, health, defense, and infrastructure workflows.
  • Both moves are positioned as key steps in advancing Leidos’ NorthStar 2030 strategy and reshaping its business mix.

Leidos, a major contractor across defense, intelligence, civil, and health markets, is using the ENTRUST acquisition to add more exposure to energy infrastructure work. That places NYSE:LDOS in the middle of ongoing grid modernization, utility reliability, and critical infrastructure resilience efforts that many governments and utilities are prioritizing.

The OpenAI partnership points to a heavier use of AI in day to day missions for government and infrastructure clients, from workflow automation to decision support. For investors, these moves are essentially about where Leidos wants its revenue mix and capabilities to be by 2030, and how AI and energy infrastructure could influence that path.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Leidos Holdings by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Leidos Holdings.

Why Leidos Holdings could be great value

For investors, the ENTRUST deal and OpenAI alliance point to Leidos pushing deeper into two areas that many of its government and utility customers care about right now: grid reliability and AI-powered productivity. The roughly US$2.4b all-cash acquisition, partly funded with a US$1.4b bridge facility, increases Leidos’ exposure to regulated utility work. The OpenAI partnership is aimed at embedding generative and agentic AI in everyday workflows across defense, civil and health programs, where peers like Booz Allen Hamilton and Science Applications International are also active.

Advertisement

How this fits the Leidos Holdings narrative

The news lines up with the existing narrative that Leidos is leaning into AI integration and acquisitions to improve efficiency and support higher quality contracts. Management is also using deals like ENTRUST to shift the business mix toward more recurring, service-based work, which the narrative frames as helpful for earnings visibility and contract win rates across core federal and infrastructure customers.

Key risks and rewards investors are weighing

  • The ENTRUST acquisition increases Leidos’ presence in utility and infrastructure engineering, which could deepen relationships with large, long-cycle customers.
  • Wider AI use through OpenAI’s tools may support faster product delivery and lower indirect costs for both internal teams and client projects.
  • Funding the US$2.4b deal with new debt and liquidity adds to balance sheet commitments, and analysts have already flagged that Leidos carries a high level of debt.
  • Acquisition integration and AI execution risk remain, especially as peers like RTX and Booz Allen compete aggressively for similar government and infrastructure budgets.

What to watch next

From here, you may want to track regulatory approvals and closing timing for the ENTRUST deal, how quickly Leidos wins new utility or grid-modernization work, and any commentary on AI adoption from upcoming earnings calls and investor updates. If you want to see how other investors are thinking about this story, check community narratives on the Leidos Holdings page and compare this news with the longer term thesis you already have in mind.

data.title: How Investors May Respond To American Eagle Outfitters (AEO) Exiting Quiet Logistics To Refocus On Core Brands
data.description:
• American Eagle Outfitters recently moved to wind down its Quiet Logistics subsidiary, which it bought for about US$360.00 million a little over four years ago, exiting third‑party logistics and closing several fulfillment centers while retaining the Atlanta site for its own distribution needs.
  • This retreat from supply chain services underscores management’s renewed emphasis on extracting more value from American Eagle’s existing retail brands and store base rather than expanding into adjacent logistics businesses.
  • We’ll now explore how exiting Quiet Logistics and refocusing on core lifestyle brands could reshape American Eagle Outfitters’ investment narrative.

These 13 companies survived and thrived after COVID and have the right ingredients to survive Trump's tariffs. Discover why before your portfolio feels the trade war pinch.

Advertisement

What Is American Eagle Outfitters' Investment Narrative?

For American Eagle Outfitters, the core investment case still comes back to believing the company can squeeze more earnings out of a relatively mature revenue base, through disciplined inventory, tighter cost control and better productivity from its existing stores and brands. Recent guidance upgrades for 2025 support that near term earnings focus, but the decision to wind down Quiet Logistics slightly reshapes the story: instead of testing adjacent profit pools in logistics, management is doubling down on its lifestyle brands and its own distribution footprint. In the short term, the Quiet exit looks more operational than transformational, with limited obvious impact on key catalysts like comparable sales trends, margin execution and capital returns, although any related closure costs could weigh on reported results. The more immediate risk remains execution in a slow growth, highly competitive apparel market.

However, investors should be aware that execution missteps may matter more now that growth is modest. Despite retreating, American Eagle Outfitters' shares might still be trading 19% above their fair value. Discover the potential downside here.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Seven Simply Wall St Community fair value views span roughly US$10 to just under US$29 per share, reflecting very different expectations, while the Quiet Logistics exit keeps the spotlight squarely on how efficiently American Eagle can run and grow its core brands.

Explore 7 other fair value estimates on American Eagle Outfitters - why the stock might be worth less than half the current price!

data.title: Is DICK'S Sporting Goods (DKS) Pricing Reflecting Long Term Gains Or Recent Pullback
data.description:
• If you are looking at DICK'S Sporting Goods and wondering whether the current share price offers solid value, this article will walk you through what the numbers actually say rather than the headlines.
  • The stock closed around US$202.00 recently, with returns of 2.0% over the last 30 days, a 1% gain year to date, a 13.9% decline over the last year, and a 60.2% and 210.4% gain over the last 3 and 5 years respectively.
  • Recent coverage around DICK'S Sporting Goods has focused on how investors are weighing those longer term gains against the more recent 1 year pullback, as sentiment around retailers has shifted over different time frames. This mix of short term caution and longer term strength is an important backdrop when you think about what a fair price for the stock might look like.
  • On our checklist driven valuation framework, DICK'S Sporting Goods scores 3 out of 6 for value, giving it a 3/6 valuation score. Next we will look at how traditional methods such as P/E, cash flow and asset based metrics line up with that, before finishing with a way of thinking about valuation that can help you make sense of all these different signals.

Find out why DICK'S Sporting Goods's -13.9% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: DICK'S Sporting Goods Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes estimates of the cash a business may generate in the future and discounts those cash flows back to today to arrive at an estimated value per share.

For DICK'S Sporting Goods, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections in $. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $293.0 million. Analysts provide inputs out to 2027, with free cash flow for that year assumed at $522.8 million, and Simply Wall St extrapolates this further. By 2035, the projected free cash flow in the model is $468.4 million, with each year discounted back to today.

Putting all of these discounted cash flows together gives an estimated intrinsic value of about $69.74 per share. Compared with the recent share price around $202.00, the DCF output implies the stock is 189.7% overvalued on this set of assumptions.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests DICK'S Sporting Goods may be overvalued by 189.7%. Discover 868 undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for DICK'S Sporting Goods.

Approach 2: DICK'S Sporting Goods Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like DICK'S Sporting Goods, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand for how much investors are paying today for each dollar of current earnings, which is often easier to relate to than cash flow models.

What counts as a normal or fair P/E usually reflects two big things: how fast earnings are expected to grow and how risky those earnings are. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk tends to point to a lower one.

DICK'S Sporting Goods is currently trading on a P/E of about 17.8x. That sits below the Specialty Retail industry average of roughly 19.5x and well below the peer group average of about 35.4x. Simply Wall St also calculates a Fair Ratio for the stock at around 20.9x, which is the P/E it might typically trade on given its earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk characteristics.

This Fair Ratio is usually more tailored than a simple comparison to peers or the sector because it folds in company specific factors instead of relying on broad group averages. With the current 17.8x P/E sitting under the 20.9x Fair Ratio, this lens points to the shares looking undervalued on earnings.

Result: UNDERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1417 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your DICK'S Sporting Goods Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Let us introduce you to Narratives, a simple tool on Simply Wall St's Community page. With Narratives, you connect your story about DICK'S Sporting Goods to the numbers by setting your own assumptions for future revenue, earnings, margins and fair value. You can then compare that fair value to the current price to help decide when you might buy or sell. The platform keeps updating your Narrative as new news or earnings arrive. As a result, one investor might build a Narrative that assumes a much higher fair value for the stock than another investor who uses more cautious assumptions and arrives at a much lower fair value, even though both are looking at the same company.

Do you think there's more to the story for DICK'S Sporting Goods? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: Siemens’ Automation and Energy Storage Push Might Change The Case For Investing In Siemens (XTRA:SIE)
data.description:
• In late January 2026, Siemens was highlighted across several industry reports for advancing robotics for data centers, AI-driven construction analytics, innovative electric control panels, and modular battery energy storage systems.
  • These developments underscore how Siemens is embedding automation, AI, and grid-support technologies across infrastructure and energy markets, reinforcing its role as a key technology enabler.
  • We’ll now examine how Siemens’ push into modular battery energy storage and broader automation solutions shapes the company’s investment narrative.

This technology could replace computers: discover 22 stocks that are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

What Is Siemens' Investment Narrative?

For Siemens, the core belief you need to have as a shareholder is that industrial automation, software and electrification will keep tightening their grip on global infrastructure, and that Siemens can keep selling higher‑value systems into that shift. The latest news on robotics for data centers, AI construction analytics, intelligent control panels and modular battery storage fits neatly into that story, but is unlikely to change the near term financial picture on its own. In the short run, the more material angles look like continued execution in software‑rich automation and disciplined capital allocation, including the ongoing share buyback and steadily rising dividend. The flip side is that Siemens already trades on a premium earnings multiple, carries meaningful debt and has seen margins slip, so expectations are not low if growth in these newer areas takes time to show up.

However, investors should also weigh how Siemens’ high valuation and debt could amplify any slowdown. Siemens' shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued by 14%. Find out what it's worth.

Exploring Other Perspectives

The Simply Wall St Community’s 11 fair value estimates span about €208 to nearly €299, reflecting very different expectations. Set that against Siemens’ push into AI automation and energy storage, and you can see why many readers may want to stress test their own assumptions before deciding how much of the story they believe.
